commands.review_summary:
The PR derives `memory_search`'s outer deadline from `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs` for QMD searches and adds fake-timer regression tests for slow and stalled QMD searches.

PR surface: Source +27, Tests +90. Total +117 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current `main` wraps `memory_search` in a fixed 15-second deadline while the QMD manager uses `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs`; the linked report supplies a 46.7-second QMD rerank trace, though I did not run a live QMD binary.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A maintainer or contributor needs to fix the canonical timeout normalization mismatch and provide live QMD proof; this is not a safe repair-lane candidate while external real behavior proof remains the merge gate.

target.title: fix(memory-core): honor qmd search timeouts
target.body:
Closes #91947

## Summary

- Derive the `memory_search` outer deadline from `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs` when QMD is configured above the default 15s tool guard.
- Keep builtin memory search, wiki-only search, and default QMD behavior on the existing 15s guard.
- Add regression coverage for a slow-but-successful QMD search and a never-settling QMD search.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed:
`memory_search` had a fixed 15s wrapper deadline, so QMD query-mode searches with `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs` set higher could still fail at 15s before QMD's configured search budget was honored.

Real environment tested:
Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `codex/memory-search-qmd-deadline`, using the repository Vitest runner and existing memory-core test mocks.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts` passed: 1 test file, 18 tests.
- `oxfmt --check` passed for both changed files.
- `git diff --check` passed with no whitespace errors.

Observed result after fix:
A mocked QMD search configured with `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs: 60000` can complete after 16s without the outer `memory_search` guard aborting it. A never-settling QMD search with the same config still returns `memory_search timed out after 65s`, preserving a finite wrapper deadline with bounded setup overhead.

What was not tested:
I did not run a live QMD binary or local reranker model; the regression is covered with the repository's memory-core unit/mocked runtime tests.
target.author: yu-xin-c
